Home › Lifestyle Medicine › Library
Evidence-Based Resources

Lifestyle Medicine Library

Articles written by the physicians at Forward Family Medicine covering the six pillars of lifestyle medicine — nutrition, physical activity, sleep, stress management, social connection, and avoiding risky substances.

Cardiovascular Prevention

  • Diet and Exercise for Heart Health: A Practical Guide The evidence-based combination of diet and exercise for cardiovascular risk reduction — what types of exercise, how much, and which dietary patterns have the strongest evidence for heart disease prevention.
  • How to Lower Blood Pressure Naturally: Lifestyle Interventions with Evidence Lifestyle changes can lower blood pressure by 10–20 mmHg in many patients. The DASH diet, sodium reduction, exercise, and weight loss all have strong clinical trial evidence.
  • How to Lower Cholesterol Naturally: Evidence-Based Strategies Evidence-based dietary and lifestyle strategies to lower LDL cholesterol — fiber, plant sterols, omega-3s, exercise, and weight loss — with specific magnitude of effect for each intervention.
  • Understanding ApoB and LDL: A Patient-Friendly Explanation ApoB is a more accurate predictor of cardiovascular risk than standard LDL. Learn what ApoB measures, how it differs from LDL cholesterol, and what your numbers mean for your heart health.

Lifestyle Medicine

  • Lifestyle Medicine in Practice: Patient Success Stories De-identified case examples showing how lifestyle medicine interventions reverse prediabetes, reduce blood pressure, improve energy, and reduce medication burden in real patients.
  • Lifestyle Medicine vs. Conventional Medicine: Key Differences Lifestyle medicine and conventional medicine share the same goals but differ in approach. Understand the structural and philosophical differences, and why the two work best together.
  • The Six Pillars of Lifestyle Medicine Explained The six pillars of lifestyle medicine — nutrition, physical activity, sleep, stress management, avoidance of risky substances, and positive social connection — explained with the evidence behind each.
  • What Is Lifestyle Medicine? A Patient's Guide Lifestyle medicine uses evidence-based behavioral interventions to prevent, treat, and reverse chronic disease. Learn what it includes, who practices it, and how it differs from conventional care.
  • When Medication Is Still Needed — And How Lifestyle Medicine Fits Lifestyle medicine isn't anti-medication. Learn when medications remain essential, how lifestyle changes reduce medication burden, and how an integrated approach works best.

Metabolic Health

  • CGM and Metabolic Health: How Continuous Glucose Monitoring Helps Continuous glucose monitors (CGMs) originally designed for diabetics are now used by non-diabetics to understand metabolic responses to food, exercise, and sleep. Learn how CGMs work and what the data means.
  • Diet Strategies for Blood Sugar Control: What the Evidence Shows Evidence-based dietary approaches to blood sugar control — low-carb, Mediterranean, low-glycemic, fiber, and timing strategies with study citations and practical guidance.
  • How to Reverse Prediabetes: Evidence-Based Strategies Prediabetes is reversible. The Diabetes Prevention Program showed lifestyle changes reduce diabetes progression by 58%. Learn the specific dietary, exercise, and lifestyle strategies that work.
  • What Is Insulin Resistance? Causes, Signs, and What to Do Insulin resistance means your cells don't respond efficiently to insulin, forcing your pancreas to produce more. Learn the causes, warning signs, tests, and how to reverse it.

Nutrition

  • How to Read Food Labels: A Practical Guide to Making Better Choices Nutrition labels can inform but also mislead. Learn how to read ingredient lists, nutrition facts, and marketing claims to make genuinely better food choices.
  • Plant-Based Eating and Health: What the Evidence Shows Plant-based dietary patterns are associated with lower rates of cardiovascular disease, type 2 diabetes, and all-cause mortality. A balanced look at the evidence, nuances, and how to start.
  • The Anti-Inflammatory Diet: What It Is and What the Evidence Shows Chronic inflammation drives heart disease, diabetes, cancer, and autoimmune conditions. An anti-inflammatory dietary pattern can meaningfully reduce inflammatory markers — here's what the evidence shows.
  • The Mediterranean Diet: What the Evidence Actually Shows The Mediterranean diet has more clinical trial evidence than any other dietary pattern. A comprehensive look at the PREDIMED trial, Lyon Diet Heart Study, and what the Mediterranean diet actually consists of.

Physical Activity

  • Exercise for Longevity: What the Research Shows About Living Longer VO2max and muscle mass are the two strongest physical predictors of longevity. Learn what the research shows about exercise and lifespan, and what to prioritize.
  • Getting Started with Exercise: A Beginner's Guide to Building a Sustainable Habit How to start exercising when you're sedentary or returning after a long break — evidence on habit formation, safe progression, and building a routine that fits your life.
  • HIIT vs. Steady-State Cardio: What the Evidence Shows Both HIIT and steady-state cardio improve health through different mechanisms. A balanced, evidence-based comparison to help you choose the right approach for your goals and situation.
  • Strength Training and Metabolic Health: Why Muscle Is Your Metabolic Organ Skeletal muscle is the primary site of glucose disposal. Strength training builds metabolic capacity, improves insulin sensitivity, reduces visceral fat, and lowers cardiovascular risk.
  • Zone 2 Training: What It Is, Why It Matters, and How to Do It Zone 2 training — low-intensity, steady-state aerobic exercise — builds the aerobic base that underpins VO2max, metabolic flexibility, and longevity. A patient-friendly guide to the science.

Sleep & Hormones

  • Practical Sleep Improvement Strategies: A Step-by-Step Guide Evidence-based, actionable strategies to improve sleep quality and duration — CBT-I, sleep restriction therapy, relaxation techniques, and environmental optimization.
  • Sleep and Hormone Regulation: How Poor Sleep Disrupts Your Body Sleep directly regulates cortisol, growth hormone, leptin, ghrelin, insulin, and sex hormones. Learn how sleep deprivation disrupts the hormonal balance that controls weight, stress, and metabolic health.
  • Sleep and Metabolic Health: How Sleep Deprivation Drives Weight Gain and Diabetes Risk Sleep deprivation reduces insulin sensitivity, increases hunger hormones, and raises diabetes risk. Learn the mechanisms and why improving sleep is a legitimate metabolic health strategy.
  • Sleep Hygiene and Long-Term Health: Why It Matters More Than You Think Sleep hygiene practices — consistent schedule, dark cool room, screen limits, caffeine timing — have measurable effects on sleep quality, hormone regulation, and long-term chronic disease risk.

Stress & Mental Health

  • Burnout: Signs, Health Consequences, and Recovery Strategies Burnout is a clinical state with measurable physiological consequences. Learn to recognize it, understand its health impacts, and implement evidence-based recovery strategies.
  • Mindfulness and Behavior Change: The Evidence and the Practice Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR) has clinical evidence for reducing anxiety, depression, chronic pain, and stress-related health outcomes. Here's what the research shows and how to start.
  • Practical Stress Interventions: What Actually Works Evidence-based stress interventions — exercise, sleep, social connection, CBT techniques, and relaxation — with practical instructions and clinical evidence for each approach.
  • Stress Physiology and Chronic Disease: How Stress Makes You Sick Chronic stress activates the HPA axis and sympathetic nervous system in ways that drive inflammation, metabolic dysfunction, and cardiovascular disease. Here's how the mechanisms work.
Put It Into Practice

Lifestyle Medicine as Part of Your Care

Forward Family Medicine is the only DPC practice on the Main Line with in-person, board-certified Lifestyle Medicine integrated into membership. No extra cost.

Schedule a Free Meet & Greet

The Main Line's only Direct Primary Care practice with in-person, board-certified Lifestyle Medicine.

Forward Family Medicine is a Direct Primary Care practice. Membership is not insurance. We recommend pairing DPC with a high-deductible or health-sharing plan.
